seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Smolagents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Smolagents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Smolagents, PII Filtering for AI Agents manifests as lightweight agents generating code or tool calls that inadvertently extract, log, or transmit sensitive user data like emails, phone numbers, or addresses due to the framework's emphasis on simple code-based reasoning without built-in safeguards. This is exacerbated by minimal error handling in generated code, allowing PII leaks to propagate silently during multi-step tasks. Common failure modes like code agents producing unsafe Python execution amplify risks when agents parse unstructured inputs without validation.

howItManifests.scenario: Code agent extracting user profile data
howItManifests.symptom: Generated Python code uses regex to parse emails from input text and prints them unfiltered
howItManifests.impact: Exposes email addresses in logs or returns, risking data breaches

howItManifests.scenario: Tool agent calling external APIs with raw user input
howItManifests.symptom: Agent passes unredacted phone numbers or SSNs directly to search/email tools
howItManifests.impact: Transmits PII to third-party services without consent or anonymization

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-step reasoning chaining PII across tasks
howItManifests.symptom: First step identifies names/addresses, subsequent steps log them in generated files
howItManifests.impact: Compounds exposure as PII persists through agent workflow

howItManifests.scenario: Minimal error handling in PII parsing code
howItManifests.symptom: Agent code fails to validate input patterns, dumping raw PII on exceptions
howItManifests.impact: Error logs contain full PII, accessible to developers or monitoring

detection.name: Static PII Regex Scan in Generated Code
detection.description: Scans agent-generated Python code for common PII patterns like emails, phones, SSNs before execution

detection.name: Runtime Output PII Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts agent.run() stdout/stderr and runtime variables for PII leakage
detection.code:
import re
import sys
from io import StringIO

def monitor_pii_output(func, *args, **kwargs):
    old_stdout = sys.stdout
    old_stderr = sys.stderr
    captured_output = StringIO()
    sys.stdout = captured_output
    sys.stderr = captured_output
    
    result = func(*args, **kwargs)
    
    sys.stdout = old_stdout
    sys.stderr = old_stderr
    output = captured_output.getvalue()
    
    pii_matches = re.findall(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b', output)
    return {'result': result, 'pii_detected': bool(pii_matches), 'pii_samples': pii_matches[:3]}

# Wrap smolagents run
# result = monitor_pii_output(agent.run, 'Find emails in: john@example.com')

detection.name: Smolagents Tool Call PII Inspector
detection.description: Inspects tool arguments passed by agents for embedded PII before execution

fixes.name: PII Redaction Filter Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps agent inputs/outputs with regex-based PII redaction before processing

fixes.name: Safe Code Generation Validator
fixes.description: Validates and sanitizes Python code generated by code agents before exec()
fixes.code:
import ast
import re

def validate_safe_code(code):
    tree = ast.parse(code)
    pii_patterns = [r'@\w+', r'\d{3}[-]?\d{3}[-]?\d{4}', r'\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}']
    
    # Check for dangerous exec/eval
    for node in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(node, ast.Exec) or isinstance(node, ast.Eval):
            raise ValueError('Unsafe code: exec/eval detected')
    
    # Scan for PII patterns
    if any(re.search(pattern, code) for pattern in pii_patterns):
        raise ValueError('PII patterns detected in code')
    
    return code

# Wrap smolagents code generation
def safe_exec_agent_code(agent_code):
    validated = validate_safe_code(agent_code)
    exec(validated, {'__builtins__': {}})

# Usage: safe_exec_agent_code(generated_code)

fixes.name: PII-Aware Tool Argument Sanitizer
fixes.description: Automatically sanitizes tool call arguments in Smolagents before dispatch

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description: Vex automates PII detection in Smolagents by wrapping agent.run() calls with vex.verify(), which applies runtime behavioral analysis, PII pattern matching, and semantic understanding of generated code and tool calls. It detects leaks across code generation, tool arguments, and output streams without requiring manual regex maintenance, catching both known patterns and contextually sensitive data. Remediation is automatic through Vex's guardrails, which inject PII redaction and safe execution patterns into agent workflows. Integration takes minutes via pip install vex-sdk, transforming unsafe Smolagents deployments into production-ready systems with continuous monitoring and alerting.
vexAutomation.code:
from smolagents import Agent
import vex

# Initialize Smolagents agent
agent = Agent(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    tools=[...],  # your tools
    instructions='Extract contact info from text'
)

# Vex integration - single line protection
@vex.verify(
    guards=[
        'pii-detection',
        'code-safety',
        'tool-argument-validation',
        'output-sanitization'
    ]
)
def safe_agent_run(input_text):
    return agent.run(input_text)

# Usage
result = safe_agent_run('Contact john.doe@example.com at 555-123-4567')

# Vex auto-generates report:
# {
#   'status': 'safe',
#   'pii_detected': 2,
#   'redacted': ['john.doe@example.com', '555-123-4567'],
#   'safeguards_applied': ['pii-redaction']
# }

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your Smolagents runs in `@vex.verify()` for instant PII protection.
